jokes.text: How is it that a moth can find its way into a triple locked titanium suitcase?! - The perils of owning cashmere.
jokes.setup: David's dramatic complaint about moths getting into his expensive luggage
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry, matter-of-fact response about cashmere problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's dramatic overreaction paired with Johnny's deadpan practicality establishes their dynamic beautifully

jokes.text: Alexis hasn't been here for a week and a half. She's at Mutt's. - Well, that's simply not true. I had breakfast with her just yesterday. - That was me!
jokes.setup: Moira insisting she had breakfast with Alexis yesterday
jokes.punchline: David revealing Moira confused him for Alexis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy that reveals Moira's self-absorption and David's exasperation. The escalation with the hosiery conversation makes it even funnier

jokes.text: We had a lengthy conversation about hosiery and menopause. - Again, that was me.
jokes.setup: The previous reveal that Moira confused David for Alexis
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd topics they discussed escalates the confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the initial confusion even more absurd - what kind of conversation about menopause did David endure?

jokes.text: Oh, I'm-I'm sorry, I don't work here. - Oh... that's-'cause you're behind the desk, and everything.
jokes.setup: Johnny claims he doesn't work at the garage
jokes.punchline: Customer's logical observation about his positioning
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that highlights Johnny's awkward position between employee and not-employee

jokes.text: Oh yeah, no, I mean I 'work' here, but I don't actually work 'here'... in the garage. I mean, I am working here in the garage, but I don't work for the garage.
jokes.setup: Customer's reasonable assumption about Johnny's employment
jokes.punchline: Johnny's increasingly convoluted explanation of his work situation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's tendency to over-explain creates a verbal knot that gets funnier as it goes on

jokes.text: Hey Johnny, I just went out and got me a muffin. - Bob, a customer was just in here, and he was saying he had a problem with a muffler. - Oh yeah? What uh, what'd it end up being? - Well, I don't know, I didn't go out and look! - Oh?
jokes.setup: Bob casually mentions getting a muffin while Johnny talks about a muffler problem
jokes.punchline: The sound similarity creates confusion, plus Johnny's admission he didn't actually help
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with muffin/muffler plus the reveal that Johnny was completely unhelpful

jokes.text: Well, I don't expect you to know everything, but you know, if a client of yours came in here, I'd certainly talk to them. - Well, I hope you wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Bob saying he'd talk to Johnny's clients
jokes.punchline: Johnny's quick retort hoping Bob wouldn't interact with his clients
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that reveals Johnny's lack of confidence in Bob's professionalism

jokes.text: Well, not that I have to worry, I know you're a little low on clients.
jokes.setup: The previous exchange about client interaction
jokes.punchline: Bob's pointed observation about Johnny's lack of business
jokes.explanation: Bob's casual cruelty lands well, especially after Johnny's earlier smugness

jokes.text: That's because I'm building a business, Bob. And right now I'm in strategy mode, looking for opportunities, and eventually I will implement a plan.
jokes.setup: Bob pointing out Johnny's lack of clients
jokes.punchline: Johnny's overly corporate, buzzword-heavy response to defend his failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny character moment - using business speak to mask the fact that he's doing nothing

jokes.text: Well, if you feel like uh, you might like to implement a muffler, be my guest.
jokes.setup: Johnny's pompous talk about 'implementing a plan'
jokes.punchline: Bob using Johnny's corporate language to mock him about the muffler
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns Johnny's pretentious language against him while bringing back the muffler

jokes.text: How do you know it's cedar? - Um, I bought a cologne once, in Japan, that's supposed to smell like the aftermath of a car crashing into a cedar tree.
jokes.setup: Stevie's simple question about wood identification
jokes.punchline: David's incredibly specific and bizarre cologne reference
jokes.explanation: Peak David Rose - the specificity of 'car crashing into a cedar tree' cologne is both absurd and perfectly in character for his pretentious yet bizarre tastes

jokes.text: Why would anyone wanna smell like that? - Hmm?
jokes.setup: David's bizarre cologne description
jokes.punchline: Stevie's logical question met with David's complete non-comprehension
jokes.explanation: Stevie's reaction grounds the absurdity while David's 'Hmm?' shows he genuinely doesn't understand why that's weird

jokes.text: A family of moths seem to have mistaken my cashmere sweaters for an Atlantic City all-you-can-eat buffet.
jokes.setup: Need to explain the moth problem
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor comparing moths eating cashmere to a casino buffet
jokes.explanation: David's flowery language creates a vivid and funny image while staying true to his dramatic character

jokes.text: You're planning on building a cedar chest? - That's correct. - You are. You're gonna build it? - It's a box! So you're just nailing some planks of wood together.
jokes.setup: Stevie's incredulous questioning of David's building abilities
jokes.punchline: David's defensive oversimplification of carpentry as 'just nailing planks together'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's mix of defensiveness and complete naivety about manual labor

jokes.text: May the force be with you. - Thank you so much. Thank you, genuinely. Honestly, you're so great.
jokes.setup: Stevie's sarcastic Star Wars reference about David's doomed project
jokes.punchline: David taking the sarcasm completely at face value and being genuinely touched
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Stevie's dry sarcasm completely missing David, who responds with genuine emotion

jokes.text: No, just an intimate breakfast, nothing special, no big 'ladies' bruncheon' for me.
jokes.setup: Moira claiming she doesn't want to be included
jokes.punchline: Her transparent passive-aggression about not being invited
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - pretending she doesn't care while making it obvious she desperately wants to be included

jokes.text: Let me guess, a bucket of shrimp in Times Square, followed by a 'Lion King' matinee!
jokes.setup: Hearing about the group's trip to New York
jokes.punchline: Moira's snobby assumption about their tourist activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira snobbery - the specificity of 'bucket of shrimp' is both accurate and condescending

jokes.text: No, actually, we performed in Central Park. - C-Central Park? Really?
jokes.setup: Moira's condescending assumptions about their NYC trip
jokes.punchline: Her shock and stammering when learning they actually performed in Central Park
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Moira's snobbery when the small-town group exceeds her expectations

jokes.text: Especially musicals. Anything 'Andy' Webber!
jokes.setup: Moira boasting about her musical theatre background
jokes.punchline: Calling Andrew Lloyd Webber 'Andy Webber'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism that undercuts her pretensions while trying to name-drop

jokes.text: Oh, yes, you'll find me on ensemble tracks seven, nine, and thirteen of the original cast recording of Starlight... 'Starlight Express.'
jokes.setup: Moira boasting about her recording career
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was just ensemble tracks on one album, and having to finish the title
jokes.explanation: Good deflation - what sounds impressive becomes much less so with specifics, plus the awkward pause before finishing the title

jokes.text: Or perhaps Jocelyn should speak to me.
jokes.setup: Twyla suggesting Moira talk to Jocelyn about joining
jokes.punchline: Moira's imperious suggestion that Jocelyn should come to her
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira entitlement delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: Yes please, I'm still not quite off book.
jokes.setup: Being offered a menu at a diner
jokes.punchline: Using theater terminology for memorizing the menu
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - applying theatrical language to mundane diner activities with complete sincerity

jokes.text: David measuring sweaters with his body while Mutt watches from the doorway
jokes.setup: David is supposedly 'measuring' for his cedar chest
jokes.punchline: He's literally using his body to measure sweaters like a human ruler
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy showing David's complete lack of practical skills. The absurdity of using your body as a measuring tool is perfectly David.

jokes.text: It's a really tragic story, involving some damaged luxury cashmere. And so I am building a cedar chest for my sweaters.
jokes.setup: Mutt asking what David is measuring
jokes.punchline: David framing moth damage as a 'tragic story' with dramatic language
jokes.explanation: David's melodramatic framing of a minor inconvenience as tragedy fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Well, how far along are you? - Um, I'm this far.
jokes.setup: Mutt asking about David's progress on the chest
jokes.punchline: David presumably gesturing to show he's made zero progress
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing David's complete lack of progress despite his confidence

jokes.text: Small minds don't reach for the stars. No more! Not on my watch. Jocelyn, I am here!
jokes.setup: Moira's frustration at not being invited to audition
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic declaration of availability with overwrought language
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira dramatics treating a small-town singing group like a major theatrical opportunity

jokes.text: Au-Audition! Uh, though I'm sure you mean no offense, in the actual world of entertainment, I'm what's known as, 'offer only.'
jokes.setup: Being asked to audition for a local singing group
jokes.punchline: Moira's horrified reaction and claim to be above auditions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira's entertainment industry pretensions are perfectly captured in 'offer only'

jokes.text: Who hasn't been?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn mentioning someone was in a regional production of Annie
jokes.punchline: Moira's dismissive response implying Annie is beneath notice
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - dismissing what should be impressive with theatrical snobbery

jokes.text: Has Paul Bunion finished his box? - Who?
jokes.setup: Stevie giving David a lumberjack nickname
jokes.punchline: David's complete confusion at the reference
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Stevie's dry mockery met with David's genuine confusion

jokes.text: Wow, this whole thing just got a lot weirder.
jokes.setup: Learning that Mutt is helping David with carpentry
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan observation about the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Stevie's reaction grounds the absurdity of the David-Mutt carpentry partnership

jokes.text: Will you be needing your basic toolbox, or your 'cedar chest' tool box? - Obviously the cedar chest tool box!
jokes.setup: Stevie offering obviously fake specialized option
jokes.punchline: David immediately falling for the non-existent specialty toolbox
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing David's gullibility and Stevie's deadpan trolling skills

jokes.text: Oh, that's in the shed. It's a big wooden box, with the words, 'tools to make a cedar chest' carved into the side of it, so it's really clear...
jokes.setup: The fake cedar chest toolbox
jokes.punchline: Stevie elaborating the fake details with complete deadpan commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise with Stevie's deadpan commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Okay, I'm assuming you're kidding. Um... But in the off chance that you're not, where in the shed would I find that box? - You're kidding.
jokes.setup: David starting to realize Stevie might be joking
jokes.punchline: But still hedging his bets and asking for location details
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing David's uncertainty even when he suspects he's being mocked

jokes.text: Great, you can call it an audition if you must, or an impromptu performance, or perhaps 'an evening with...'
jokes.setup: Agreeing to perform for the group
jokes.punchline: Suggesting increasingly grandiose titles for her performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira escalation from simple audition to full theatrical event

jokes.text: I'll wait, far be it for me to get in the way of you making any necessary improvements.
jokes.setup: Being asked to wait while they finish rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Moira's backhanded compliment implying they need lots of work
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive Moira delivered with false politeness

jokes.text: Well, first of all Bob, that's not the expression.
jokes.setup: Bob saying 'keeping busy or hardly working'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's pedantic correction of the mangled expression
jokes.explanation: Johnny's pedantic nature showing through even in casual conversation

jokes.text: You got some clients, that's great news! - No, I was dealing with your customers!
jokes.setup: Bob misunderstanding Johnny's complaint as good news
jokes.punchline: Johnny's frustrated clarification that he was doing Bob's job
jokes.explanation: Good frustration comedy showing the communication gap between Johnny and Bob

jokes.text: You know, I'm playing the guy like a bow fiddle.
jokes.setup: Johnny describing his sales technique
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'violin' with 'bow fiddle'
jokes.explanation: Mild malapropism that fits Johnny's tendency to slightly mangle expressions

jokes.text: Well, funny you should use that expression. Because Dick Sinson is gonna be riding his bike until we can get his car back for him!
jokes.setup: Johnny's pride in his sales skills and 'riding a bike' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Bob revealing the 'customer' will literally be riding a bike because Johnny sold his car
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns Johnny's metaphor literal while revealing the disaster he's caused

jokes.text: Who's Dick Simpson? - I couldn't tell 'ya. But Dick Sinson is the fella whose car you just sold.
jokes.setup: Johnny getting the customer's name wrong
jokes.punchline: Bob's correction emphasizing the mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of the mispronounced name that continues through the scene

jokes.text: Ridiculous name! - Sin-Sinson... Sinson! Dick Sin-Sinson!
jokes.setup: The ongoing name pronunciation issues
jokes.punchline: Johnny's frustrated attempts to get the pronunciation right
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the name gag showing Johnny's continued confusion and frustration

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I can't not jump in and help when I see someone doin' something wrong. - Was it wrong, or was it just unconventional?
jokes.setup: Mutt explaining why he helped with the chest
jokes.punchline: David's attempt to reframe his incompetence as creativity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's inability to admit fault even when being helped

jokes.text: If you're experiencing feelings for me, Like, I totally get it. And normally I'd be into it, I've just been down this road before, and it's-it's messy.
jokes.setup: Mutt saying he has something to tell David
jokes.punchline: David immediately assuming it's romantic feelings and pre-rejecting
jokes.explanation: Perfect David narcissism - immediately assuming any serious conversation must be about romantic interest in him

jokes.text: Well, she sort of fades into the background after a while, you know, like a smoke alarm.
jokes.setup: Mutt complaining about Alexis always being around
jokes.punchline: David's comparison of his sister to an annoying but constant presence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures both the annoyance and the background quality of an overstaying houseguest

jokes.text: Are you just saying that because you don't want her to move back here? - Yes, I am!
jokes.setup: Mutt questioning David's motives for the advice
jokes.punchline: David's immediate honest admission of self-interest
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing David's refreshing honesty about his selfish motivations

jokes.text: Oh no, we were done early, we got plenty of time. - Thank you, but I think I'm fighting a little bug.
jokes.setup: Being offered the chance to audition immediately
jokes.punchline: Moira suddenly developing a convenient illness to avoid performing
jokes.explanation: Classic character moment showing Moira's performance anxiety overriding her arrogance

jokes.text: Come on, Moira! audition! Moi-ra! Moi-ra! Moi-ra! - Stop! That's cruel!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to back out of the audition
jokes.punchline: The group chanting her name and her dramatic reaction to the 'cruelty'
jokes.explanation: Good pressure cooker moment that forces Moira into the performance she both wants and fears

jokes.text: Well, I can't say no to anyone!
jokes.setup: Being pressured into auditioning
jokes.punchline: Moira's claim about being unable to say no, when she clearly can and does
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Moira reframing her inability to resist attention as generosity

jokes.text: [Moira's entire bizarre singing performance with vocal runs and nonsense syllables]
jokes.setup: The buildup to Moira's professional audition
jokes.punchline: Her completely over-the-top, bizarre performance that's wildly inappropriate for the group
jokes.explanation: Peak Schitt's Creek cringe comedy - Moira's performance is simultaneously technically impressive and completely wrong for the situation

jokes.text: Oh, that was... different.
jokes.setup: Moira's wildly inappropriate audition performance
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's diplomatic but clearly stunned response
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement reaction that captures the awkwardness without being mean

jokes.text: Um, like, he brought all my stuff back, and I was like, this is great, but then at the same time I was just kinda like, I feel like I need my space right now.
jokes.setup: David asking about her situation with Mutt
jokes.punchline: Alexis claiming she asked for space when we know Mutt wanted her gone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Alexis has completely reframed the situation to preserve her ego

jokes.text: So... but I'm really glad that I told him, and he took it really, really well.
jokes.setup: Alexis continuing to describe 'her' decision to get space
jokes.punchline: Her relief that Mutt 'took it well' when it was actually his idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of the dramatic irony with Alexis misreading Mutt's relief completely

jokes.text: That's a cute little wood thing, David. - Thank you, I made it.
jokes.setup: Alexis noticing David's carpentry project
jokes.punchline: David's proud acceptance of credit when Mutt did most of the work
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing David taking credit where little is due
